title: Generator Fuel Level Monitoring
exclusions: Sites whose generators lack an authenticated Modbus/SNMP fuel totalizer feed, unmetered belly tanks, or dual-fuel plants where gallons telemetry does not map to the monitored circuit should exclude this UC until instrumentation parity exists.
evidence: Export the triggered alert snapshot showing `_time`, `generator_id`, `fuel_level_pct`, `fuel_gallons`, and `engine_status`, attach the paired trending panel for the preceding six hours, and file the CMMS fuel verification or dipstick reading ticket ID referenced in the incident record.

controlTest.positiveScenario: During a controlled utility failover drill the generator stays online, SNMP polls record monotonic decreases in fuel_gallons with fuel_level_pct crossing 22 while engine_status remains running, and the scheduled alert returns the generator row within the five-minute poll window.
controlTest.negativeScenario: An operator opens a maintenance window that flips engine_status to standby and pauses SNMP walks; stale cached gallons briefly satisfy the numeric predicate until ingestion resumes—tests should prove the alert stays suppressed when the maintenance lookup flag is active.

dataSources: Land **`generator:telemetry`** events on **`index=power`** after SNMP polling via **Splunk Connect for SNMP ([Splunkbase app 6266](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6266))** or the **SNMP Modular Input add-on ([Splunkbase app 1537](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1537))**, or after a BMS-forwarded Modbus register map that writes the same schema. Each event should carry extracted fields **`fuel_level_pct`**, **`fuel_gallons`**, **`generator_id`**, and **`engine_status`** so the catalog SPL thresholds and tables resolve without ad hoc renaming.
spl:
index=power sourcetype="generator:telemetry"
| where fuel_level_pct < 25
| table generator_id, fuel_level_pct, fuel_gallons, engine_status
description: Monitors normalized standby generator fuel telemetry pulled from the generator controller’s Modbus or SNMP paths and reconciled against the BMS fuel view so percent tank, totalizer gallons, and engine state stay aligned before a low-fuel breach pages anyone.
value: Leadership gains a defendable countdown to empty-tank risk during utility loss, which steers diesel vendor dispatches, customer impact calls, and load-prioritization decisions without relying on manual stick readings in a storm.
implementation: Schedule five- to fifteen-minute Modbus holding-register or SNMP OID polls from the generator controller, merge the BMS historian check for drift, normalize to `sourcetype="generator:telemetry"`, derive burn rate whenever `engine_status` shows a run, and alert when `fuel_level_pct` violates the contracted reserve window while correlating `_indextime` lag.
visualization: Primary **`splunk.singlevalue`** gauge bound to **`fuel_level_pct`** with red threshold below the contracted reserve; companion **`splunk.line`** chart plotting **`fuel_gallons`** and **`fuel_level_pct`** over `_time`; investigator **`splunk.table`** listing **`generator_id`**, **`engine_status`**, calculated burn rate, and indexed lag minutes; optional facility map overlay keyed by **`generator_id`** for multisite fleets.

references.title: Splunk Documentation — Splunk Enterprise modular inputs overview

knownFalsePositives: • **Generator controller firmware refresh** — transient SNMP timeouts can null `fuel_level_pct`; distinguish with a lookup-tagged maintenance host and suppress pages until polls succeed twice. • **Quarterly load-bank exercise** — rapid gallon deltas while **`engine_status=running`** mimic leaks; distinguish by correlating the CMMS exercise ticket window and expected burn envelope. • **BMS historian catch-up batch** — replays duplicate gallons readings; distinguish by requiring monotonic timestamps per **`generator_id`** before alerting. • **Tank refill air-pocket stabilization** — percent snaps upward then settles; distinguish by delaying alerts until readings hold for two poll cycles after the refill work order closes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Stand up collector capacity for the **BMS, generator controller Modbus/SNMP** path documented in this UC: either **Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase 6266)** profiles that walk generator OIDs or **SNMP Modular Input (1537)** stanzas on a Heavy Forwarder, plus any Modbus middleware your BMS team operates that forwards JSON to HEC—keep secrets in `storage/passwords`, not in dashboards.
• Contract `props.conf` / transforms so every event lands on **`index=power`** as **`sourcetype="generator:telemetry"`** with stable extractions for **`fuel_level_pct`**, **`fuel_gallons`**, **`generator_id`**, and **`engine_status`** before you enable alerting.
• Create a least-privilege RBAC role such as **`power_facilities_ops`** with search rights limited to **`index=power`** and grant **`schedule_search`** only to the service account that owns this detection.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll tank percent and gallon totalizer registers (Modbus function blocks) or SNMP fuel tables every **five minutes** (`cron` `*/5 * * * *` on modular inputs), stamp maintenance windows in a lookup when the generator controller firmware is upgraded, and have the BMS publish reconciliation rows so drift between building automation and the controller surfaces during QA.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the UC SPL into Search, save it as a report, then schedule an alert whose **`dispatch.earliest_time`** matches the poll cadence (for example `-10m@m`). Keep thresholds in a macro (`macros.conf`) so winter demand changes do not require editing raw SPL.

Step 3 — Validate
Spot-check **`fuel_level_pct`** and **`fuel_gallons`** against the generator controller’s local panel and the BMS fuel module for the same **`generator_id`** across a `-24h` window; confirm `_time` tracks controller time, not just forwarder receipt, before promoting the alert.

Step 4 — Operationalize & troubleshoot
Publish a dashboard with fuel gauge, trend, and residual-runtime math, document runbook actions (dispatch fuel truck, shed load), and if alerts fire during queue backlogs, inspect Heavy Forwarder pipeline metrics—treat sustained `_indextime` skew as ingestion triage, not a true low-fuel event.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
